title: I'm 45 years old and just broke the $300,000 milestone with my low-priced stock account.
author: Doub-37
selftext:
I'm 45 years old this year.



A few days ago, my US stock account balance just surpassed $300,000.



For others, this might not mean much;



But for me, it means—the days of staying up all night watching the market, enduring losses alone, and constantly learning have finally paid off.



Ten years ago, I opened my first US stock account with only $5,000.



At that time, I knew nothing about "investing," only that I had too little money to afford those large-cap blue-chip stocks.



So, I turned my attention to low-priced stocks.



High-risk areas often hide overlooked opportunities.



A few years ago, through several successful short-term trades, my account grew from $5,000 to $20,000.



That's when I truly understood—the power of compound interest stems from patience.



Some typical opportunities I seized:



A small-cap tech stock, I bought at $2, it rose from $2 to $6, and I took profits in stages.



I bought a new energy component company for $3, and it doubled in six months.



Of course, there were losses too—I bought a biotech stock for $1, and it fell to $0.40.



But I never got discouraged because I learned to control losses and maximize profits.



Out of ten trades, five were small wins, three were small losses, and two were large wins.



Ultimately, my account kept growing.



By age 45, my account had grown from a few thousand dollars to $300,000.



This wasn't due to insider information or luck,



but rather through countless analyses, stop-loss orders, patience, and self-reflection over a decade.



I know $300,000 isn't the end. I need to keep working hard.



I created a low-priced stock discussion group, completely free, open to anyone, to share information on stocks that might rise daily.



I will reply to every private message. Welcome to join!



Thank you for your comments and congratulations. Thank you to everyone who read this article.
